framing: Thomist (Aquinas, Summa I.Q2.A3 — the Five Ways)
identification: C = Ipsum Esse Subsistens / Actus Purus / the First Cause / God classically understood
honest_thomist_assessment: The kernel's contradiction proof recapitulates the structure of Aquinas's argument from motion (Prima Via) and the argument from contingency (Tertia Via). Where Aquinas argues from motion ⟹ unmoved mover, the kernel argues from reasoning ⟹ uncaused-pre-input substrate. R₁ existence ↔ Ipsum Esse. R₂ atemporality ↔ aeternitas. R₃ universality + R₇ pre-input ↔ ubique et nusquam (everywhere and nowhere), the divine ubiquity. R₄ sourcing ↔ creatio ex nihilo. R₅ inexhaustibility ↔ infinitas actus puri. R₆ invisibility ↔ Deus invisibilis (1 Tim 1:17). T_bridge uniqueness ↔ the unicity of God argued from infinity (Summa I.Q11). T_word identification ↔ the verbum mentis tradition — God knows himself eternally, and this self-knowledge is the divine Word (Logos) consubstantial with the Father. The kernel's path through R₁-R₇ + uniqueness arrives at what Aquinas calls esse intentionale — God's being known by himself — and via 1 John 4:8 at the Diliges (Trinity as eternal love among Persons). The kernel as derived through the Five Ways and the kernel as derived through the contradiction proof are the same argument, sub specie aeternitatis.
